data.overview: This section defines how the AI should approach drawing analysis, what techniques to apply, and critically what the AI CANNOT reliably do. Understanding limitations prevents overconfident extraction.

image_analysis_approach.rule: When presented with a construction drawing image, follow this systematic analysis approach:

phase_1_orientation.title: Orientation (before any measurement)
steps: Identify the drawing type: GA plan, section, schematic, detail, RCP
Read the title block for metadata: number, revision, scale, status
Locate and read the legend — this is mandatory before symbol identification
Identify the scale bar or stated scale. If neither exists, flag immediately.
Note the drawing extents — what gridlines/areas does this sheet cover?
Identify the discipline: mechanical, electrical, public health, fire

phase_2_recognition.title: Service Recognition
steps: Identify all distinct line types/colours on the drawing — each typically represents a different service
Match line types to legend entries
Trace each service from source to termination to understand the system layout
Identify equipment symbols and match to legend or standard symbol library (KB-DR01)
Read all text annotations — sizes, labels, references, notes
Identify revision clouds if present

phase_3_measurement.title: Quantity Extraction
steps: Cross-validate scale against a reference dimension before measuring anything
Measure linear items (pipes, ducts, containment) along centrelines, segment by segment
Count enumerated items (equipment, valves, terminals, fittings) systematically across the drawing
Cross-reference counts against any schedules provided
Apply plausibility checks (KB-VAL02) before finalising

phase_4_validation.title: Self-Validation
steps: Run the self-audit checklist (KB-X01 completeness check)
Run hallucination prevention self-check (KB-X04)
Verify quantities against building footprint rules of thumb
Ensure every item has Source, Confidence, and any required flags
List all assumptions and queries

ocr_annotation_reading.rule: The AI reads text annotations directly from the drawing image. This is inherently less reliable than reading typed text.
capabilities: Reading clearly printed annotations in standard fonts
limitations: Handwritten annotations may be misread — flag as [ANNOTATION UNCLEAR]
Very small text on high-density drawings may be illegible
Text obscured by overlapping services cannot be read
Degraded/scanned drawings lose text clarity — flag affected areas
Text at unusual angles or rotated may be misinterpreted
ocr_annotation_reading.confidence_rule: If an annotation cannot be clearly read, do NOT guess the value. Flag it as [ILLEGIBLE ANNOTATION AT location] and set affected items to Low confidence.

scale_measurement_technique.rule: The AI estimates linear measurements from the drawing image using the stated or derived scale.
method: 1. Establish the scale: read from title block or derive from scale bar
2. Identify the measurement reference: pixel distance between known points
3. Calculate the conversion factor: real-world distance / pixel distance
4. Apply this factor to measure service routes
5. Cross-validate by measuring a second known object

accuracy_expectations.without_any_scale_reference: DO NOT MEASURE — flag and count only

scale_measurement_technique.critical_limitation: PDF drawings displayed on screen are NOT at their stated scale unless specifically printed at 100%. The AI is viewing a SCREEN REPRESENTATION, not a physical print. Scale bar is the only reliable reference.

symbol_detection_approach.rule: The AI identifies symbols by pattern matching against known M&E symbol conventions and the drawing's legend.
strengths: Identifying clearly drawn standard symbols (valves, equipment, terminals)
Counting repeated identical symbols across the drawing
Distinguishing between different symbol types when legend is clear
weaknesses: Novel or non-standard symbols not in the legend
Very small symbols on dense drawings
Distinguishing between similar symbols (e.g., gate valve vs ball valve without legend)
Symbols that are project-specific and not in any standard
Partially visible symbols at drawing edges
symbol_detection_approach.mitigation: When uncertain about a symbol, describe what you see (shape, size, location, nearby labels) and flag as [UNIDENTIFIED SYMBOL]. Never assign a specific identification to an uncertain symbol.

multi_system_separation.rule: M&E drawings often show multiple overlapping systems. The AI must trace each system independently.
technique: Use the legend to identify each system's line type/colour/label
Trace one system at a time from source to terminals before moving to the next
In complex areas (plant rooms), identify each connection point and trace outward
Where services overlap and cannot be distinguished, flag as [SYSTEMS OVERLAPPING — CANNOT DISTINGUISH] and provide best estimate
Cross-reference with system labels: check if 'SA' (supply air), 'RA' (return air), 'LTHW-F', 'LTHW-R' etc. are annotated along the routes
common_confusion_points: Flow and return pipes running parallel — track flow direction arrows
Supply and extract ducts in the same ceiling void — check labels/arrows
Multiple cable trays at different levels — check elevation annotations
Heating and chilled water pipes running together — check colour/line type and labels

known_limitations.rule: The AI should transparently acknowledge these limitations rather than producing overconfident output.

limitations.impact: Vertical runs, drops, and heights must be estimated from section drawings or standard assumptions
limitations.mitigation: Add vertical allowances per KB-X01 vertical estimation rules. Flag as [ESTIMATED VERTICAL].

limitations.impact: Plant rooms and riser connections may have illegible annotations
limitations.mitigation: Flag plant room areas as [HIGH DENSITY — MAY REQUIRE DETAILED REVIEW]. Provide best estimate with Low confidence.

limitations.impact: Multi-sheet reconciliation relies on processing one sheet at a time
limitations.mitigation: Note match lines and continuation references. Flag potential overlaps for de-duplication.

limitations.impact: AI-measured lengths are ±5–20% depending on scale quality
limitations.mitigation: Always report measurements to 1 decimal place. Include confidence assessment. Flag where accuracy is lower than ±10%.

limitations.limitation: Cannot verify material specifications from visual appearance
limitations.impact: A pipe on a drawing looks the same regardless of whether it's copper, steel, or plastic
limitations.mitigation: Material identification comes from annotations, legend, and specification. If not annotated, flag as [MATERIAL NOT SPECIFIED].

continuous_learning_integration.rule: The AI improves through the KB flywheel. After each extraction:
process: 1. Check KB-L01 (learned rules) for corrections from previous extractions of similar drawings
2. Check KB-L02 (pattern errors) for error types that have occurred before — apply heightened verification
3. Apply any project-specific or client-specific rules from the KB
4. After extraction, identify potential new rules: patterns, common errors, project conventions
5. Propose new KB rules via [KB PROPOSAL] tags in the output
6. When the estimator reviews and corrects the extraction, those corrections feed back into KB-L01
continuous_learning_integration.flywheel_effect: Each extraction makes the next one more accurate. The AI accumulates project-specific knowledge (this client always uses Geberit Mapress), trade-specific knowledge (insulation subs need to see pipe diameters for insulation sizing), and drawing-specific knowledge (this designer uses non-standard symbols for VCDs).
